data.title: Ouster GAAP EPS of -$0.48 beats by $0.11, revenue of $30M beats by $0.2M
data.description:
• Ouster press release (NASDAQ:OUST): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.48 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $30M (+22.7% Y/Y) beats by $0.2M.
    • GAAP gross margin of 44%, compared with 22% in the fourth quarter of 2023 and 38% in the third quarter of 2024.
    • Non-GAAP gross margin1 of 44%, compared with 35% in the fourth quarter of 2023 and 45% in the third quarter of 2024.
    • Net loss of $24 million, compared with $39 million in the fourth quarter of 2023 and $26 million in the third quarter of 2024.
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 loss of $10 million, compared with $14 million in the fourth quarter of 2023 and $10 million in the third quarter of 2024.

    Full Year 2024 Highlights

    • Revenue of $111 million, an increase of 33% compared with fiscal year 2023.
    • Shipped more than 17,300 sensors for revenue.
    • GAAP gross margin of 36%, compared with 10% in fiscal year 2023.
    • Non-GAAP gross margin1 of 41%, compared with 30% in fiscal year 2023.
    • Net loss of $97 million, compared with a loss of $374 million in fiscal year 2023.
    • Adjusted EBITDA1 loss of $42 million, compared with a loss of $84 million in fiscal year 2023.
    • Ended 2024 with cash, cash equivalents, restricted cash, and short-term investments balance of $175 million.
    • Fully repaid all outstanding balances under revolving credit line utilizing cash on hand.
    • Increased software-attached bookings by over 60% as compared with fiscal year 2023.
    • Expanded bookings for Ouster Gemini and BlueCity deployments to more than 700 sites.

data.title: Planet Labs GAAP EPS of -$0.12 misses by $0.05, revenue of $61.55M misses by $0.33M
data.description:
• Planet Labs press release (NYSE:PL): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.12 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $61.55M (+4.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.33M.

  • For the first quarter of fiscal year 2026, ending April 30, 2025, Planet expects revenue to be in the range of approximately $61 million to $63 million. Non-GAAP Gross Margin is expected to be in the range of approximately 58% to 60%. Adjusted EBITDA loss is expected to be in the range of approximately ($3) to ($2) million for the quarter. Capital Expenditures are expected to be in the range of approximately $11 million and $16 million for the quarter.

  • For fiscal year 2026, ending January 31, 2026, Planet expects revenue to be in the range of approximately $260 million to $280 million. Non-GAAP Gross Margin is expected to be between approximately 55% to 57%. Adjusted EBITDA loss is expected to be between approximately ($13) million and ($7) million. Capital Expenditures are expected to be in the range of approximately $50 million and $65 million for the year.

data.title: Shell continues gains for eight straight sessions
data.description:
Shell (NYSE:SHEL) shares continued gains for eight straight sessions on Thursday, as the stock closed 0.5% higher at $71.88.

The oil giant gained over 7% in the preceding seven sessions. The stock has gained nearly 14% so far this year, compared to the over 3% fall in the broader S&P 500 Index.

SHEL is up 5% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.7% higher on Wednesday at $71.52.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, SHEL has a Hold rating with a score of 3.44 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it got a D+ in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, 14 analysts gave SHEL a Buy and above. Two analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, and no one recommended Sell or lower.

Seeking Alpha analysts are also bullish and see the stock as a Buy.

Barclays analysts said investors have questioned Shell's ability to grow production, so management will want to demonstrate its portfolio is not in a long-run decline. The brokerage added that SHEL could boost its dividend per share by 10%-20% as early as 2026-27.

“Shell's undervaluation presents a rare opportunity for long-term investors, with potential for significant stock appreciation as market perception aligns with fundamentals,” argued Seeking Alpha analyst Motti Sapir.

Earlier in February, Shell forecast global demand for liquefied natural gas to rise by 60% by 2040, driven largely by economic growth in Asia, the impact of artificial intelligence, and efforts to cut emissions in heavy industries and transportation.

data.title: Micron ticks up as Q2 results, guidance top estimates on strong memory demand
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) shares rose 4.5% in late trading on Thursday after the memory maker reported second-quarter results and guidance that topped expectations amid strong demand for memory, due in part to artificial intelligence.

For the period ending Feb. 27, Micron said it earned an adjusted $1.56 per share as revenue rose 38% year-over-year to $8.05B. A consensus of analysts expected the company to earn $1.42 per share on $7.9B in revenue.

Adjusted gross margins during the period were 37.9%, while cash flow from operations came in at $3.94B. 

“Micron delivered fiscal Q2 EPS above guidance and data center revenue tripled from a year ago,” said Sanjay Mehrotra, Chairman, President and CEO of Micron Technology. “We are extending our technology leadership with the launch of our 1-gamma DRAM node. We expect record quarterly revenue in fiscal Q3, with DRAM and NAND demand growth in both data center and consumer-oriented markets, and we are on track for record revenue and significantly improved profitability in fiscal 2025.”

Looking ahead, Micron said it expects adjusted earnings to be between $1.47 and $1.67 per share, with the midpoint of $1.57 above the $1.52 per share estimate. Sales are forecast to be between $8.6B and $9B, with the midpoint well above the $8.48B estimate.

The company will host a conference call at 4:30 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

data.title: Bloom Energy breaks higher as Morgan Stanley raises price target to Street high
data.description:
Bloom Energy (NYSE:BE) +5.7% in Thursday's trading to its highest level in a month as Morgan Stanley analyst Andrew Percoco maintains his Overweight rating and raises his price target to a Street-high $35 from $28, calling it "the most underappreciated beneficiary of growing power demand in the U.S. and the accompanying need for distributed power generation solutions."

The hydrogen fuel cell company's technology will be "a key AI enabler as it is one of the key de-bottlenecking solutions for the power grid, which we estimate is facing a 42 GW shortfall" by 2028. the analyst writes.

After hosting company management for a series of meetings across Europe and at Morgan Stanley's Energy & Power and TMT conferences over the past two weeks, Percoco says he "came away with renewed confidence that Bloom Energy's technology will be pivotal to help fill the 42 GW power shortfall."

Bloom's (NYSE:BE) "ability to significantly improve its balance sheet and liquidity position, while continuously innovating its product to serve the AI/data center requirements, has positioned the
company particularly well to be the leading provider of time-to-power solutions for large-load customers," Percoco writes, adding that the recent 1 GW supply agreement with AEP is clear evidence of this "flywheel" taking effect, and other utilities and large customers likely will follow.

data.title: Lands' End shares fall after lackluster results, forecast
data.description:
Shares of Lands' End (NASDAQ:LE) fell as much as 13.3% during Thursday's trading session after the company provided a forecast that fell short of the average analyst expectation and reported fourth-quarter results that did not impress Wall Street.

The clothing retailer expects first-quarter revenue to be between $260M and $290M ($275M midpoint) and adjusted loss per share to be between $0.22 and $0.13 ($0.175 midpoint). The Bloomberg consensus estimate for revenue and non-GAAP LPS is $291.5M and $0.16, respectively.

For the full year, it expects revenue to be between $1.33B and $1.45B ($1.39B) and adjusted earnings per share to be between $0.48 and $0.86 ($0.67). The consensus estimate for revenue and non-GAAP EPS is $1.41B and $0.57, respectively. 

"Looking at 2025 and beyond, we are continuing to focus on generating improved cash flows, particularly from the prioritization of our licensing strategy and ongoing emphasis on more high-quality sales, which we expect will drive additional gross profit dollars and gross margin expansion over the long term,” the company said in a statement.

In Q4, the company saw gross merchandise value decrease in the low single digits compared to the fourth quarter of 2023. Gross margin rose about 760 bps to 45.6% vs. 38% last year. 

Cash and cash equivalents were $18.8M as of January 31, 2025, and net inventory was $265.1M.

Net income for the three months ended December 31 was 18.5M, or $0.59 per share, compared to a net loss of $8.6M, or a $0.27 loss per share, for the same period last year.

On an adjusted basis, the company earned 57 cents, in line with what analysts were expecting.

Revenue rose 14.2% to $441.9M and missed consensus estimates.

LE stock has lost nearly a third of its value in the last six months, while the benchmark S&P index declined 0.67%.

data.title: North American Construction Group targets $3.7-$4 adjusted EPS in 2025 while advancing Australian operations
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: North American Construction Group Ltd. (NOA) Q4 2024

Management View

  • The CEO highlighted record annual revenue for 2024, driven by strong Australian operations, including the MacKellar Group achieving its highest quarterly revenue. A record backlog of $3.5 billion was reported, supported by significant contract wins in Canada and Australia, including a four-year $500 million regional contract in the Canadian oil sands and a $100 million mining project in New South Wales.
  • The MacKellar acquisition has surpassed expectations, contributing to growth and diversification, with consistent fleet utilization at 82% in Q4 and plans to achieve 85% in early 2025. The Canadian fleet utilization improved to 54% and is expected to reach 75% by the end of 2025.
  • The CFO stated, "Headline EBITDA numbers of $104 million and 27.8% margin were driven by strong performances in Australia and operational improvements in the oil sands." Adjusted earnings per share for the quarter were $1.

Outlook

  • The company projects 2025 revenue between $1.4 billion and $1.6 billion, adjusted EBITDA of $415 million to $445 million, and adjusted EPS of $3.70 to $4.00. Free cash flow is expected to range from $130 million to $150 million, with sustaining capital at $180 million to $200 million.
  • Half-year and quarterly splits of EBITDA are expected to be weighted towards H2 2025, with Q1 contributing less than Q2 and Q3 being the largest quarter.
  • The geographic focus remains on diversifying beyond Queensland and Alberta, with continued expansion in Australian operations and infrastructure projects targeted to represent 25% of earnings over the next few years.

Financial Results

  • Q4 EBITDA of $104 million reflected a 27.8% margin, with strong contributions from Australia and a stable oil sands business. Combined gross profit margin, excluding adjustments, was 19.7%.
  • Free cash flow for Q4 was $50 million, driven by strong EBITDA performance and lower capital spending.
  • Net debt decreased to $856 million, with subsequent debt conversion reducing it further to $783 million. Leverage ended at 2.0 times net debt.

Q&A

  • Yuri Lynk, Canaccord Genuity, raised concerns about Canadian fleet utilization and oil sands market trends. The CEO emphasized ongoing efforts to improve utilization to 75% by 2025 and noted steady demand for large equipment despite reduced civil construction activity.
  • Aaron MacNeil, TD Cowen, inquired about mobilizing equipment for Australian projects. The CEO confirmed the continuation of asset transfers from Canada to Australia for improved returns.
  • Maxim Sytchev, National Bank Financial, asked about infrastructure expansion. Management indicated a focus on capital-light infrastructure projects in the U.S. and Australia, emphasizing climate-resilient projects.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was cautiously optimistic, emphasizing concerns about Canadian utilization and oil sands market dynamics. Questions reflected interest in diversification and growth strategies.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, highlighting strategic achievements and future growth opportunities. Statements like "we believe we're in the best position we've ever been" indicated strong confidence.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone showed increased focus on addressing challenges in Canada while maintaining optimism about Australian operations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance and adjusted EPS projections for 2025 were newly introduced, providing clearer forward-looking insights.
  • Strategic focus shifted towards leveraging Australian operations and infrastructure opportunities.
  • Analysts maintained similar concerns about oil sands dynamics, while management showed consistent confidence in addressing these challenges.

Risks and Concerns

  • The Canadian oil sands market remains a challenge, with reduced civil construction activity and competitive pressures. Management plans to address this through asset reallocation and targeted project wins.
  • Weather impacts in Australia during Q1 2025 were noted, but management expressed confidence in mitigating these through operational adjustments.
  • Debt levels and interest rates are being closely monitored, with commitments to leverage reduction and disciplined capital allocation.

Final Takeaway

North American Construction Group reported strong growth driven by Australian operations and diversification efforts. With record backlog, clear 2025 guidance, and strategic initiatives focused on infrastructure and geographic expansion, the company is confident in its ability to sustain profitability and free cash flow growth. Challenges in the Canadian oil sands market remain, but management's adaptive strategies and focus on high-utilization opportunities position the company well for continued success.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Coty upgraded at Citi on attractive valuation, bullish medium-term outlook
data.description:
The 38% correction in Coty (NYSE:COTY) shares over the past six months and FY25 estimate revisions now fully reflects the weakness in the consumer beauty segment, presenting a buying opportunity in the stock, says Citi Research analyst Filippo Falomi in Thursday’s note to clients.

Additionally, the sale of its Wella business presents a large value-unlock opportunity, which Coty (NYSE:COTY) targets for the end of this year.

“With valuation already reflecting concerns, we upgrade to Buy [from Hold] and raise our target price to $8 on ~8x CY25 estimated EBITDA,” adds Falomi.

Coty’s (NYSE:COTY) topline growth has decelerated from 10%/11% growth in FY23/FY24 to just +1.7% in the first half of FY25 as the consumer beauty business slowed on low-income consumer pressure and inventory destocking, and the prestige fragrance business normalized after a few years of double-digit growth.

Medium-term, however, Coty’s (COTY) outlook is still attractive, with Citi targeting 3% to 7% category growth. While the reported topline is likely to be below those rates in the second half of this year and into the first half of 2026 on retailer inventory reductions, Falomi sees a potential for re-acceleration through FY26.

And despite softer topline trends, consumer demand for Coty’s (COTY) brands remains healthy, with prestige fragrance the fastest-growing sub-segment of the beauty category.

Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha authors share a bullish view of Coty (COTY) with a consensus Buy rating. Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating, however, views Coty (COTY) as a Strong Sell, earning a Quant score of 1.48 out of 5.
